description: Define a consistent spacing and sizing scale using mathematical ratios so components are visually balanced, grouping is clear, and whitespace reinforces information hierarchy.
rules: Start with too much white space — conservative spacing always looks better than cramped layouts
Establish a spacing and sizing system — use a base unit (4px, 8px) and multiply by scale (1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12...)
You don't have to fill the whole screen — avoid stretching content to viewport width; use containers
Grids are overrated — align by rhythm and intent, not arbitrary grids
Relative sizing doesn't scale — use absolute units in system, scale responsively in breakpoints
Avoid ambiguous spacing — every gap between elements should be intentional and expressed in system

outcomes.result: spacing scale is defined and enforced across all components

given: two elements are adjacent in interface
outcomes.result: no ambiguous spacing; every gap is intentional
content is readable and respects typography measure
visual grouping is clear without explicit borders or backgrounds

fields.name: base_unit
fields.description: Foundation spacing unit in pixels (4px or 8px)

fields.name: container_max_width
fields.description: Maximum content width in pixels (calculated from type measure)

related.description: Spacing reinforces hierarchy through grouping and visual weight
Type measure determines ideal container width for readability
Spacing scale scales responsively across viewport breakpoints
Spacing system is applied within container layout patterns
